key_insight: A unified DVAE + particle filter framework can learn latent weld-pool dynamics and monitor arc welding robustly across different processes without process-specific tuning.
engineering_relevance: This enables more reliable real-time quality monitoring for welding lines by reducing sensitivity to arc radiation, reflections, and other disturbances.
method: CNN-based encoder-decoder learns compact latent features from weld-pool images.
LSTM transition model captures time-evolving latent dynamics under control inputs.
Particle filter tracks latent and hidden states online to suppress sensor noise and preserve process history.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports validation on both GTAW and GMAW, showing generalizability and robustness without process-specific tuning.
watch_item: Performance may still depend on image quality and the particle filter’s computational cost for real-time deployment.
paper_title: Robust Monitoring of Arc Welding Processes: A Generalizable Framework with DVAE and Particle Filter
